title: Podman Windows v4.2.1 msi not able create api forwarding
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
after installing podman v4.2.1 using the msi on windows 10 with wsl2 install. I get the following error when running podman machine start "API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\podman-machine-default: The system cannot find the file specified."
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. install podman msi

2. run 'podman machine init'

3.run 'podman machine start'

**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman.exe version 4.2.1
```

comments.body: Do you see any errors, what does podman start output?

interesting, now I get this error when running 'podman machine start' $ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/podman-machine-default

Another process was listening on the default Docker API pipe address.
You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER HOST using the
following powershell command in your terminal session:

        $Env:DOCKER_HOST = 'npipe:////./pipe/podman-machine-default'

Or in a classic CMD prompt:

        set DOCKER_HOST = 'npipe:////./pipe/podman-machine-default'

hmm... unable to pull image with podman, but can with docker cli  "podman run -dt -p 8080:80/tcp docker.io/library/httpd
Trying to pull docker.io/library/httpd:latest...
Error: initializing source docker://httpd:latest: pinging container registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry-1.docker.io: Temporary failure in name resolution"
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@rhatdan I have the same issue. Running podman 4.3.0. It seems to randomly cycle between this error and the following output:

API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: All pipe instances are busy.

I'm guessing some other application or service is using that pipe, but I don't know what application that would be.

@n1hility I have Docker Engine (without the Desktop option) installed, but the daemon is not running. Is there a way to determine which application is doing the binding, or perhaps force all applications to relinquish that resource? Or, if all else fails, make podman use another pipe?

@TheRealHnefi sysinternals process explorer will tell you in most cases. You can search for open handle for "docker_engine" . However, it's also possible that the failure is a temporary false alarm and the actual binding was successful. You can try to do a "docker info" for example and if it returns successfully with podman info in the output it is actually working. 

One thing to check would be the proxy process is binding ok on the listening side

```
Get-WinEvent -ProviderName '.NET Runtime' -Oldest
```

The pipe busy error can also just mean "not ready", and there isn't a way to tell the difference. Currently podman will wait up to 20 seconds for it to become available (as long as you are on 4.3 or later), before giving up and reporting the last error.

Let me know what you see.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing assuming this is no longer an issue. Feel free to reopen or create a new issue if there are more details to share.
